channel.title: bothSidesHeadlinesNLbreakingNews
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from bothSidesHeadlinesNLbreakingNews, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Emory Professors Sue University Over Handling of 2024 Campus Protest Arrests
item.description: Three Emory University faculty members have filed a legal complaint alleging the institution violated its own free speech policies during a campus demonstration related to the conflict in Gaza.

item.title: Late-Night Congressional Sessions Draw Criticism Over Legislative Dysfunction
item.description: All-night voting sessions have become a recurring pressure tactic used by leadership in both major parties, raising concerns about the efficiency of the legislative process.

item.title: Rights Groups Issue Travel Advisory for U.S. Ahead of FIFA World Cup
item.description: Amnesty International and several U.S. civil rights organizations have released a formal advisory cautioning World Cup visitors about what they describe as a deteriorating rights environment.

item.title: Florida Zoo Welcomes Baby Koala in New Australian-Themed Habitat
item.description: The Palm Beach Zoo has two milestones to mark with the opening of its new Outback exhibit, which includes a young koala currently residing in its mother's pouch.

item.title: Iran's Foreign Minister to Visit Pakistan as U.S. Moves on Energy Trade Policy
item.description: Iran's top diplomatic official is expected to arrive in Pakistan for talks by the weekend, Pakistani officials confirmed, as the U.S. takes steps affecting regional oil and gas flows.
